description_long: This is a mid-rise office building with a symmetrical facade, featuring a grid of evenly spaced rectangular windows across its white exterior. The front entrance is accentuated by an arched stone surround, giving prominence to the main entryway. The building has a flat black roof with a single rooftop structure and a visible chimney or vent. The rear of the building is finished in a contrasting brown brick texture, with fewer windows and a more utilitarian appearance. The side elevations are narrow, with the same window grid pattern as the front, and the overall structure is boxy and regular. The building's design suggests early 20th-century commercial architecture, with a focus on function and repetitive window bays.
description: A boxy, mid-rise office building with a white grid facade, arched main entrance, and a flat black roof. The rear is finished in brown brick with fewer windows, contrasting with the regular window pattern on the front and sides.
description_view_0: The front view shows the symmetrical white facade with a grid of windows and a prominent arched main entrance at street level.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the building's narrow profile, flat roof, and the continuation of the window grid pattern.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a brown brick finish with fewer windows, highlighting a more utilitarian and less decorative side of the building.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the narrow profile, flat roof, and window grid, with the rooftop structure visible.
attribute_window_pattern: Regular grid of rectangular windows, evenly spaced in horizontal and vertical rows across all facades except the rear, which has fewer windows.
